title: US extends sanctions waiver on Russian crude with China angle - what it means for India
content: Earlier today petroleum ministry joint secretary Sujata Sharma said that India will continue importing Russian crude based on commercial practicality and its energy security requirements, regardless of whether US sanctions waivers are in place.

title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI
content: A jury in Oakland ruled Elon Musk waited too long to sue OpenAI and its leaders, a decision that significantly benefits the AI company and its founders. The jury found Musk's lawsuit barred by the statute of limitations, a finding the judge indicated she agrees with. This ruling comes as OpenAI prepares for a potential IPO.

title: Twisha death case: CCTV shows her heading to terrace; husband, others attempt CPR later
content: A chilling CCTV video shows Twisha Sharma ascending to her terrace shortly before her death in Bhopal.  Her husband, Samarth Singh, is absconding, with a reward offered.  The case has taken a turn as the mother-in-law, a retired judge, claims Twisha had psychiatric issues and substance abuse problems, contradicting dowry harassment allegations.

title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei makes a 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad: CSK's 'Arjuna' who refuses to attack
content: Among all batters who have faced at least 100 balls in the powerplay this season, Gaikwad has the lowest strike rate — 124.6. For comparison, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has scored at 244.51 and Abhishek Sharma at 228.87.

title: Salesforce CEO who halted hiring sees company spending millions on Anthropic tokens
content: Salesforce is significantly boosting AI spending, projecting hundreds of millions on Anthropic tokens, while halting software engineer hiring. CEO Marc Benioff highlights AI's role in enhancing engineer productivity by over 30%, enabling faster output and lower development costs. This strategic shift reflects a broader tech trend of leveraging AI for efficiency, with humans still integral to operations.

title: In God We Trump: MAGA's Project Big MAC unfolds on the Mall
content: A recent prayer event in Washington, backed by the White House, has sparked debate about religion and nationalism. Thousands gathered for 'Rededicate 250,' with speakers advocating for America to be a Christian nation. This event highlights a trend of merging American identity with Christianity, a move critics liken to a 'Make America Christian' project.

title: Watch: Dhoni enters field at Chepauk for final home appearance of IPL 2026
content: MS Dhoni thrilled Chepauk fans with a brief on-field appearance during CSK’s final home game of IPL 2026 against Sunrisers Hyderabad, despite missing the match through injury. The emotional moment came during the mid-innings team photo. Earlier, Ravi Shastri confirmed through Ruturaj Gaikwad that Dhoni was still unfit, while CSK managed 180/7 batting first.

title: What is Iran’s ‘Hormuz Safe’, insurance platform for Strait of Hormuz shipping
content: Iran has introduced 'Hormuz Safe', a digital insurance platform. Commercial ships can now use this system to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Payments are accepted in cryptocurrency like Bitcoin. This initiative aims to manage the waterway commercially. It is expected to generate significant revenue for Iran. The platform ensures secure cargo transits through cryptographic verification.

title: Taiwan & Thucydides Trap: How close have the US and China come to conflict before?
content: Taiwan, a flashpoint in US-China rivalry, is increasingly central to global tensions. President Xi Jinping's "Thucydides Trap" warning highlights the risk of conflict as China rises.  The island's strategic importance in semiconductors and trade, coupled with historical crises, underscores the escalating danger of miscalculation.

title: Raaj Kumar called everyone ‘Jaani’ after his dog, recalls filmmaker
content: Filmmaker KC Bokadia shared anecdotes about working with the eccentric Raaj Kumar, finding him more challenging than Amitabh Bachchan. Bokadia recounted casting Raaj Kumar for 'Police Aur Mujrim' just a day before the shoot, securing his agreement with a slightly increased fee and a request to join immediately.

title: Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf net worth 2026: Earnings and investments
content: Steffi Graf and Andre Agassi are a very recognizable tennis pair, and they have an estimated net worth in 2026 of $290 million. They’ve become wealthy by winning Grand Slams, signing multiyear contracts and other investments. Both benefited from the brand equity, business pursuits and financial planning that they developed during and after their successful professional tennis careers.

title: FIFA 2026 controversy erupts as fans slam BTS World Cup song credits
content: Fans are criticizing BigHit Music after BTS was reportedly credited on Jungkook’s solo FIFA World Cup song Dreamers. Many argued that Jungkook alone created and performed the track, calling the move unfair to his solo achievements. The backlash grew amid fresh buzz about BTS potentially performing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show, reigniting conversations about solo recognition, group branding, and BigHit’s marketing strategy surrounding Jungkook’s global success.

title: NFL trade rumours: Chiefs warn Bills after defence-heavy draft strategy
content: The Kansas City Chiefs are generating major excitement after loading up on defensive talent in the 2026 NFL Draft. NFL analyst Steve Muench believes Kansas City could produce the league’s Defensive Rookie of the Year after aggressively rebuilding its secondary. Following key departures in the defensive backfield, the Chiefs are expected to rely heavily on young defenders as they prepare for another AFC battle with teams like the Buffalo Bills.

title: 'Twisha confessed': Mother-in-law's big drugs during pregnancy charge; says parents disowned her
content: Noida woman Twisha Sharma was found dead at her marital home in Bhopal, with her mother-in-law alleging psychiatric issues and drug use. Twisha's family claims dowry harassment and murder, refusing the body and demanding a second post-mortem. Police are investigating and have announced a reward for her absconding husband.

title: ‘Waiver or no waiver’: 6 reasons why India will continue to buy Russian crude
content: India remains the largest buyer of Russian seaborne crude, with imports touching near-record levels in April and May following previous sanctions-related relaxations.

title: 'It doesn't matter whether Dhoni plays': Manjrekar gets brutally honest
content: Despite missing IPL 2026 due to multiple injuries, MS Dhoni remained the centre of attention for CSK. Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar criticised the franchise for becoming too emotional over Dhoni instead of focusing on results and team balance. While praising Dhoni’s legacy, Manjrekar said CSK must prioritise performances and reality over sentiment moving forward.

title: Oil above $100: How soaring crude prices are hitting India — explained in 10 charts
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are significantly impacting India's economy. Rising crude oil prices have led to a Rs 3 per litre fuel hike, eroded investor wealth by over Rs 5.85 lakh crore, and pushed the rupee to an all-time low. Foreign investors are also withdrawing funds, while the import bill has surged.

title: Revealed: How Asim Munir courted US military and Trump
content: A resurfaced Pakistani diplomatic cable alleges US involvement in Imran Khan's 2022 ouster. The document, citing a meeting with a US official, suggests American concerns over Khan's Russia stance and hints that his removal via a no-confidence vote would mend ties. Khan's party views this as proof of foreign interference in Pakistan's politics.

title: Key accused in Bengal CM Suvendu aide's murder arrested in UP's Muzaffarnagar
content: On May 11, a Special Investigation Team (SIT) probing Rath's murder had arrested three people before widening its probe across multiple states. The cops suspected that hired sharpshooters were brought in from outside West Bengal to carry out the killing with military-style precision. A key breakthrough came after investigators traced a UPI payment made at the Nivedita Setu toll plaza on May 6.

title: Karnataka’s Dubare elephant camp tragedy: 5 rules to follow in presence of wildlife
content: A tourist tragically died at Dubare Elephant Camp after being caught between two fighting elephants during a bathing session. This incident highlights the dangers of close wildlife encounters, even with trained animals, and underscores the need for strict adherence to safety guidelines around wildlife.

title: Roger Federer and Mirka Federer combined net worth 2026
content: By 2026, Roger Federer and Mirka Federer’s combined net worth will be one of the largest financial empires among athletes in sports history. The wealth has been built up by ATP prize money, record-breaking endorsement deals and strategic investments like On Running, showing the enduring brand power of tennis. Lifestyle management, family operations, and business direction are also big players in the game, along with Roger Federer and Mirka Federer.

title: Twisha dowry death case: Absconding husband's bail plea rejected; Rs 10k reward for info
content: A Bhopal court denied anticipatory bail to lawyer Samarth Singh, accused of abusing his wife Twisha Sharma, whose recent death has sparked dowry harassment claims. Police have announced a Rs 10,000 reward for information leading to Singh's arrest, with efforts underway to prevent him from fleeing the country. His bail plea alleged Sharma was a drug addict undergoing psychiatric treatment.

title: Your landlord is refusing to return your security deposit, here's what you can do
content: Tenants facing issues with security deposit refunds after vacating flats can find recourse. Landlords cannot arbitrarily withhold funds; deductions must be for genuine damages or unpaid dues. The Model Tenancy Act proposes limits on deposits. If disputes arise, tenants should document everything, send formal notices, and consider mediation before legal action.

title: 'Complex & severe': China just issued a warning the world shouldn't ignore
content: China's economic recovery is facing headwinds as factories slow and consumer spending falters, with April data revealing a significant slowdown. Weak domestic demand, coupled with a challenging global environment marked by geopolitical conflicts and rising energy costs, is squeezing growth. While exports remain strong, they are insufficient to offset internal weaknesses, prompting concerns about the need for further policy support.

title: 'Dhoni, where is he?': Shashtri sends Chepauk crowd into frenzy- Watch
content: Former India coach Ravi Shastri sent Chepauk into a frenzy during the toss by asking Ruturaj Gaikwad if MS Dhoni would play against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The CSK skipper confirmed Dhoni is “not fit enough” due to a calf strain. The must-win IPL 2026 clash carried huge playoff implications for both CSK and SRH.

title: ‘Smiling Buddha’ at 52: Test that announced India’s nuclear arrival
content: Fifty-two years ago, India detonated a nuclear device in Rajasthan's Thar desert, codenamed 'Smiling Buddha'. This event, coinciding with Buddha Purnima, marked India's entry into the nuclear club. Despite being officially termed a 'peaceful nuclear explosion', the test ignited debate and reshaped India's global standing, demonstrating scientific self-reliance and announcing strategic intent.

title: Venkat Prabhu gifts GOAT plate to CM Vijay; fans hail ‘prediction’
content: Director Venkat Prabhu presented Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay with the 'TN07 CM 2026' number plate from the film 'The Greatest of All Time'. This gesture comes after a scene from the 2024 movie, featuring Vijay with the same registration, went viral, with fans now viewing it as a political prediction for the 2026 elections.

title: Pakistan deploys squadron of JF-17 jets, 8,000 troops to Saudi Arabia amid US-Iran war
content: Pakistan has deployed 8,000 troops, fighter jets, and an air defense system to Saudi Arabia under a mutual defense pact. This substantial, combat-capable force aims to support the kingdom amid rising regional tensions. The move follows Iran's submission of a revised peace proposal through Pakistan, as a fragile ceasefire with the US remains under strain.

title: Parliamentry panel summons NTA chief after NEET paper leak; reform in focus
content: ​NTA chairperson Pradeep Kumar Joshi and other bureaucrats have been summoned for deliberations on May 21. The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education, Women, Children, Youth and Sports will seek the views of top education ministry bureaucrats on the alleged paper leak and reforms of NTA, which holds centralised admission tests to various undergraduate courses, on May 21, a Rajya Sabha notice said according to PTI.

title: Dak Prescott rumour: Caitlin Rance breaks silence amid Sarah Ramos silence
content: Dak Prescott, the Dallas Cowboys’ star player and popular quarterback, seemed to have been in a pretty peaceful and happy relationship with his former fiancee, Sarah Jane Ramos, as the two planned their lavish Lake Como wedding on 10th April, after having two children together. But just weeks before the wedding, Sarah Jane Ramos called off the wedding as reports surfaced of his infidelity. As Dak Prescott is now linked to Sarah Jane Ramos’ former bridesmaid, Caitlin Rance, she has now broken her silence.

title: First look: Indian Railways reveals proposed bullet train design
content: India's first bullet train, destined for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad route, has had its design unveiled by Indian Railways. This ambitious project, spanning 508 km, is progressing with station foundation work completed in Gujarat and ongoing construction in Maharashtra, including an undersea tunnel. The initiative aims to boost indigenous high-speed rail capabilities.

title: NEET paper leak case: Privilege motion moved against Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan may have to face privilege proceedings in Rajya Sabha. Congress leader Jairam Ramesh submitted a notice, accusing Pradhan of lowering Parliament's dignity with remarks about a parliamentary committee post the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Ramesh stated the minister's comments showed contempt for Parliament and its committees.

title: Meet Noronha: A 4 PM CSK call, UAE roots, and a Yuvraj tribute with 6 sixes in an over
content: Dubai-raised Karnataka all-rounder Macneil Hadley Noronha’s IPL dream finally came true after an emotional 4 PM call from Chennai Super Kings. The 24-year-old’s journey spans 14 years of struggle, family sacrifices across Dubai and Bengaluru, standout domestic performances and a memorable six-sixes tribute to his idol Yuvraj Singh.

title: 'Unhe Fortuner chahiye thi': Kin claim Noida woman beaten, thrown off terrace over dowry
content: A 24-year-old woman in Greater Noida allegedly died after being thrown from her house terrace, with her family accusing her husband and in-laws of dowry harassment. The family claims she was beaten and injured with a sharp object before being pushed to stage an accident. Police are investigating the incident.

title: Salman Khan's ex girlfriend Sangeeta Bijlani gushes over his shirtless picture
content: Salman Khan's recent shirtless photo and introspective caption about loneliness sparked fan concern. Adding to the buzz, ex-girlfriend Sangeeta Bijlani's fiery comment on the post evoked nostalgia. The former couple, who were once close to marriage, maintain a friendly relationship. Salman is currently preparing for upcoming projects, including one inspired by the Galwan Valley clash.

title: India is now Maoist-free, declares Shah, ‘with pride’
content: Home Minister says the country is largely free from security challenges of Jammu & Kashmir, the Northeast, and Naxalism; he urges the people of Bastar to “accept” about 3,000 surrendered Naxals and help them become part of the mainstream

title: Focus shifts to tussle within  AIADMK ahead of district  secretaries’ meeting with EPS
content: A cross-section of the party functionaries feels that Mr.  Palaniswami should follow up on his statement calling on the dissidents to agree to talks with an appeal for reunification

title: FIR against government officials for not doing Census work
content: A complaint by a Census official alleges the employees concerned defied government orders by not collecting data and not making a single entry in the designated house listing operation (HLO) mobile app, despite repeated warnings to begin their work

title: U.S. Justice Department drops all charges against Gautam Adani; case closed permanently
content: The case turned in Adani’s favour after prosecutors found no clear U.S. linkages and insufficient evidence to sustain the allegations, according to people familiar with the matter

title: 38 million full-time jobs may fall if war in West Asia continues, warns ILO
content: The report warned that if fuel or fertilizer prices rise, or shortages persist, the effects could extend beyond farm incomes to food prices, rural livelihoods and food security, in countries that depend on imported fertilizers, including India

title: Development works worth ₹1 crore sanctioned for water conservation: MLA
content: Launching tank development works in Orvakal mandal, Ms. Charitha Reddy says the government is taking various measures to conserve water resources in rural areas and enhance groundwater levels

title: Cut foreign travel, use more EVs: Finance Ministry’s ‘austerity’ steps for public sector banks & insurers
content: Circular does not mention the West Asia crisis, but it comes a little more than a week after Prime Minister Narendra Modi exhorted the nation to adopt similar austerity measures.

title: Palghar tempo-truck collision: 13 people die; CM Fadnavis announces compensation
content: An tempo packed with more than 50 passengers heading for an engagement ceremony collided with a speeding container truck near Dhaniwari village

title: Modi in Norway highlights: PM holds interaction with Norwegian business leaders
content: PM Modi was conferred with Norway's highest civilian honour, the Grand Cross of the Royal Norwegian Order of Merit

title: Church bodies offer to broker Kuki-Naga peace in Manipur
content: Leaders of two church organisations meet Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh in a bid to end the conflict between the two communities in the State

title: Chandrababu Naidu congratulates TTD on near 90% forest cover in Tirumala hills
content: Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams reaches 89.40% green cover in Seshachalam Hills; Chief Minister hails effort to protect forest wealth and restore native species

title: No religious sloganeering, pelting of stones will be allowed, Suvendu warns protesters
content: ‘I am giving a clear message that there will be zero tolerance towards this kind of unrest, hooliganism and anti-national, anti-social activities,’ the newly elected West Bengal Chief Minister says

title: Parliament standing Committee to review the reforms of National Testing Agency
content: The agenda includes a review of the implementation of the K. Radhakrishnan Committee report on NTA reforms, as well as an update on the investigation into the alleged NEET-UG paper leak case

title: 10 killed in accident as van collides with truck in Lakhimpur Kheri
content: The van was going to Sisaiya from Lakhimpur when the accident took place between Unchgaon and Bharehta villages

title: Bail is the rule is not an empty slogan, even in UAPA and ‘terror’ cases: Supreme Court
content: The court says the threshold for denying bail under the UAPA is so low that the state has only to show that the accusations were ‘prima facie’ true. The trial may continue for years and pre-trial incarceration begins to acquire a post-trial punitive character, it points out

title: Samajwadi Party to mobilise PDA voters by highlighting their absence from governance structure
content: The audit aims to highlight the discrimination these social groups face when officials are selected for important assignments like District Magistrate and Superintendent of Police

title: Supreme Court asks CBI to furnish classified documents to former R&AW officer in Official Secrets Act case
content: The court was hearing a petition filed by Major General (retd.) V.K. Singh, who had alleged irregularities within the intelligence agency in his 2007 book

title: U.S. nuclear industry delegation meets Jitendra Singh, explores private investment avenues in India
content: Jitendra Singh told the delegation that India aimed to scale up nuclear capacity from the current 8.8 GW to 100 GW by 2047 — a roughly 12-fold expansion that forms a key plank of  ‘Viksit Bharat’ vision

title: Telangana conducts State-level mock exercises to enhance disaster preparedness
content: Exercise aims to strengthen disaster response systems amid increasingly unpredictable climatic conditions that have led to sudden heavy rains, flash floods and forest fires, besides major industrial accidents in recent years

title: Who is Abhijeet Dipke? Inside the viral Cockroach Janta Party movement
content: What began as a satirical response to Chief Justice of India Surya Kant’s oral observation has grown into an online movement reflecting youth anger, political satire, and distrust in institutions

title: Bakrid to be celebrated in Kerala on May 28
content: Id-ul-Adha is observed worldwide on the 10th day of Dul Haj, coinciding with the culmination of the annual Haj pilgrimage in Mecca.

title: Major rail rejig leaves SCR leaner, Kazipet division case stronger
content: South Central Railway will be left with just three divisions instead of the current six, following the formation of the new South Coast Railway (SCoR) zone with Visakhapatnam as its headquarter

title: IISc study says Namma Metro Blue Line can run entirely on solar power
content: The researchers say the project represents the first corridor-scale techno-economic analysis in India examining direct integration of solar power into a metro rail traction system

title: Rising fuel prices: Modi government lacked foresight, kept things ‘hidden’ ahead of elections, says Kharge
content: The Congress leader accuses the Centre of pushing fuel price hikes till after the polls to avoid electoral backlash

title: Farmers’ groups demand withdrawal of Bidadi township project, oppose acquisition of 9,640 acres
content: Leaders of the organisation, along with affected farmers, say the project was ‘anti-farmer’ and environmentally destructive

title: The politics of family affairs in the Kerala Cabinet
content: Several members of the V.D. Satheesan Cabinet which took charge on May 18 come with strong family legacies in Kerala politics

title: Watch: Clock is ticking for Iran, says Trump | Above the Fold | 18.05.2026
content: In today’s episode: Trump’s latest warning raises fears of new U.S. strikes on Iran and escalating tensions in West Asia. We also cover PM Modi’s talks with Nordic leaders, V.D. Satheesan taking oath as Kerala’s new Chief Minister, and more.

title: Settipalli lands: Registration documents distributed to beneficiaries
content: Ministers Ponguru Narayana and Anagani Satya Prasad laud Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu for resolving the issue that has been pending for over 70 years

title: US Drops All Charges Against Gautam Adani, Case Closed Permanently
content: With this, multiple US regulatory and legal investigations involving the group have all closed in the last couple of days.

title: Ahmedabad Jewellery Heist Ends In Betrayal, Woman Staff Arrested
content: The accused planned the jewellery heist with her accomplice and fled the city with him following the theft. However, things took a turn after the accomplice abandoned her at a Delhi hotel with a lion's share of the stolen ornaments.

title: 2-Month-Old Girl Among 7 Dead In Truck-Autorickshaw Collision In Bihar
content: Seven people, including a two-month-old girl, died and four were injured when a truck collided head-on with an auto-rickshaw in Bihar's Khagaria district on Monday, a police officer said.

title: Pune Man Pours Acid On Wife's Private Parts After Affair Suspicion: Cops
content: A man working as a welder allegedly thrashed his 25-year-old wife and poured floor cleaning acid on her private parts in Pune district on suspicion that she was having an affair, police said on Monday.

title: Vijay Pays Tribute To Ex-LTTE Chief Prabhakaran On Death Anniversary
content: Global Lankan Tamil population and sections of Tamils in India observe May 18 as the Mullivaikal remembrance day, or Tamil Genocide Remembrance Day.

title: Kerala Flood Survivor Hugs Rahul Gandhi, Blesses VD Satheesan At Oath Event
content: Seethalakshmi Amma had come to Thiruvananthapuram with two wishes: to witness VD Satheesan take the oath as chief minister and to embrace him.

title: Video Shows 3 Men Bringing Twisha Sharma's Body Down Staircase
content: The video obtained by NDTV shows Twisha Sharma heading towards the terrace of the house in the Katara Hills area. Twisha, 33, was found dead at her husband's house. Samarth Singh, a lawyer, is currently on the run.

title: US Iran War News Live Updates: "Clock Is ticking": Trump Warns 'Nothing Will Be Left' Of Iran Without Deal
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: Woman Dies While Saving Her 3 Children From Fire In UP
content: A woman lost her life while rescuing her three children from a fire that broke out in a multi-storey building in Uttar Pradesh's Prayagraj district, officials said on Monday.

title: PM Modi Meets Norway King In Oslo, Highlights India's Rapid Transformation
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday called on King Harald V of Norway and highlighted India's rapid transformation and the emerging opportunities it offers for Norwegian businesses.

title: Key Accused In Suvendu Adhikari Aide's Murder Arrested From UP
content: The CBI has taken over the probe into Chandrakant Raths murder from the West Bengal Police following a request from the state government.

title: Relief For Adani Group, US Sanctions Probe Ends In $275 Million Settlement
content: Adani Enterprises Ltd (AEL), the flagship firm of the conglomerate helmed by billionaire Gautam Adani, "agreed to pay USD 275 million to settle its potential civil liability for apparent violations of OFAC sanctions on Iran".

title: Enough Petrol, Diesel Stocks: Centre After US Waiver On Russian Oil Expires
content: Sujata Sharma, the Joint Secretary of the petroleum ministry said, "There is sufficient stock of petrol, diesel in the country. No dryout is reported at any LPG dealership".

title: Man, Daughter Die After Getting Locked In Burning Car In Gujarat
content: The family was traveling toward their native village of Ranjitpar when the moving sedan suddenly sparked and caught fire, likely triggered by a short-circuit.

title: Coimbatore Horror: Boy, 13, Killed And Buried Allegedly By School Friends
content: Investigators said the boys, after realising their friend had died, allegedly buried the body at the spot and returned home, fearing arrest.

title: Weather Office Warns Of Heatwave Across Northwest, Central India This Week
content: "No significant change in maximum temperatures is likely over the rest of the country till May 24," the department added.

title: Oil Prices Dip After Report Of US Suspending Iranian Crude Sanctions
content: The unconfirmed report by Iranian media cited an unnamed source, but analysts and traders seized on the news.

title: Bengal Forms Panels To Probe "Cut Money" Claims, Violence Against Women
content: Announcing the decision on Monday, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said both inquiry committees will commence their work on June 1.
